# MSFS 2024 Controller Visualizer {{VERSION}}

## Highlights
- First public release of `MSFS 2024 Controller Visualizer`.
- Beta release for early users who want an easier way to review their Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 controller bindings.
- Designed for regular MSFS users who export their controller profiles from the simulator and want a clear visual layout of mapped buttons and switches.

## What this release includes
- Support for loading exported MSFS 2024 controller profile XML files.
- Automatic discovery of supported controllers from the folder you select.
- Visual rendering of controller-specific layouts with your current in-sim bindings.
- Print support so you can keep a reference sheet near your setup.

## Supported controllers
- Honeycomb Alpha Flight Control
- Honeycomb Bravo Throttle Quadrant
- Saitek Pro Flight Rudder Pedals
- MFD Cougar Display (Left and Right)

## Before you start
- This app works with exported profile XML files from MSFS 2024.
- You must export your controller profile(s) from the simulator before opening them in the app.
- For best results, export all profiles for the same device into one folder.

## Installation
1. Download `MSFSControllerVisualizer-{{VERSION}}.zip` from the release assets.
2. Extract the archive to a folder of your choice.
3. Run `MSFSControllerVisualizer.exe`.
4. In the app, click `Browse...` and select the folder that contains your exported MSFS 2024 controller XML files.

## Beta notes
- This is an early beta release and may still have rough edges.
- Only the controllers listed above are currently supported.
- If your controller is not listed, the app will not render a layout for it yet.

## Feedback
- If you find a bug, open a bug report in GitHub.
- If you want support for another controller, open a `New Controller Request` issue and attach the exported XML profile file(s).
